notes: Prospective transfer students interested in majoring in philosophy are strongly encouraged to complete two lower-division philosophy courses, or their articulated equivalents, prior to transferring: PHIL 9 – Introductory Symbolic Logic One additional lower-division philosophy course Refer to the "Recommended Preparation" section below for courses approved to apply to each of the requirements listed above. Students who cannot enroll in the articulated equivalent of PHIL 9 at a California Community College are strongly encouraged to take PHIL 9 at UC Santa Cruz before their first fall quarter. PHIL 9 is offered online during a 10-week summer session. For more information, please visit the UC Santa Cruz Summer Edge webpage . Additional Information for Transfer Petitioning Courses: Transfer students who have completed courses in the topics listed below, but which are not included in the "Recommended Preparation" section, may petition to have these courses count toward the second lower-division philosophy course requirement: Introduction to Symbolic Logic Ancient Philosophy Modern Philosophy Ethical Theory Philosophy of Mind or Epistemology Students should consult with the philosophy advisor about petitioning courses prior to enrolling in their first quarter. Please visit the Philosophy website to learn more about this major.

All transfer applicants must satisfy University of California admissions eligibility requirements and campus admission selection criteria. All admission requirements must be completed by the end of spring prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility requirements and admission to UC Santa Cruz, please visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that ensures your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

The UC Santa Cruz Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate (IB), and A-Level examination credit policies may be found in the UC Santa Cruz General Catalog . AP, IBH, and A-level exams may or may not satisfy course requirements, depending on the specific exam.

This is a non-screening major: completion of specific major coursework is not required for admission. Although not required for admission, students are strongly encouraged to complete as many lower-division preparatory courses as possible from among those listed in the “Recommended Preparation” section before transferring to UC Santa Cruz. Doing so will help you make timely progress toward degree completion. Additionally, completing the Intersegmental General Education Transfer Curriculum (IGETC) is encouraged, especially for non-screening majors in the Humanities, Arts, and Social Sciences Divisions. While not required for admission, IGETC completion will facilitate timely graduation. For more information on incorporating IGETC into your academic plan, please consult with your community college counselor.
